metadata_information.title: The Financial Costs of the U.S.-China Trade Tensions : Evidence from East Asian Stock Markets
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: The Financial Costs of the U.S.-China Trade Tensions : Evidence from East Asian Stock Markets

document_description.abstract: This paper examines the impacts of U.S.-China trade tensions via the lens of East Asian stock markets. Studying 10 indices of the main East Asian stock markets, it finds that announcements of "trade war" escalation translated into 50 to 60 percent of the total declines in two major Chinese stock markets over the first eight months of 2018. In other words, in the absence of the "trade war" Asian stocks would have experienced half the decline, or they would have registered gains.
document_description.spatial_coverage: Rest Of The World ; East Asia and Pacific
